title: This legislative session, Assemblymember Zbur is proud to announce the successful advancement of ten bills to the Governor's Desk. Among these is the California Retail Theft Reduct

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California Retail Theft Reduction Act addresses the rise in organized retail theft by implementing new measures to combat retail crime.",
    "The bill creates a new crime targeting 'serial' retail thieves with a penalty of up to three years for possession of stolen property with intent to resell.",
    "The California Retail Theft Reduction Act allows the value of thefts from different victims to be aggregated to reach the threshold for grand theft.",
    "The bill expands the use of diversion and rehabilitative programs like drug court for shoplifting and petty theft.",
    "The Climate Resiliency Bond will support initiatives for safe drinking water, drought and flood resilience, and wildfire management.",
    "$475 million will be allocated to advance offshore wind energy as part of the Climate Resiliency Bond.",
    "AB 2442 establishes an expedited licensure process for healthcare providers offering gender-affirming care in response to anti-LGBTQ+ legislation.",
    "The Jacqueline Marie Zbur Rare Disease Advisory Council aims to address the unique challenges faced by Californians with rare diseases.",
    "AB 2907 enhances gun safety protections for survivors of domestic violence, stalking, and elder abuse.",
    "AB 2917 strengthens California's Gun Violence Restraining Order system by focusing on hate-based threats against protected groups.",
    "AB 2704 decreases financial barriers for prospective In-Home Supportive Services providers by reducing background check fees."
  ]
}
```
